title: 1.1: Tests that the screen line buffer returns rendered line contents when drawing text inside active margins under width mode changes.
1.2: Tests that the screen line buffer returns visible buffer contents after repeated drawing advances past a bottom margin.
1.3: Tests that the screen line buffer returns visible buffer contents after repeated drawing advances with a top margin set.
1.4: Tests that the screen line buffer returns visible buffer contents after repeated drawing advances within top margin bottom margin constraints.
2.1: Places the test for margin behavior under width modes in kitty_tests/screen.py.
2.2: Places the test for bottom margin behavior in kitty_tests/screen.py.
2.3: Places the test for top margin behavior in kitty_tests/screen.py.
2.4: Places the test for simultaneous margin behavior in kitty_tests/screen.py.
3.1: All tests use the BaseTest framework assertion helpers for state comparison within screen behavior validation.
3.2: All tests follow the test_<behavior> naming pattern reflecting the screen margin scenario under validation.
3.3: Each newly added margin test initializes its own Screen instance via `self.create_screen(...)` within the test.
4.1: The DECCOLM/margins test includes at least one assertion that compares rendered text (e.g., `str(s.line(...))` or `str(s.linebuf)`) to an expected value.

4.2: The bottom-margin test asserts that scrolling affects scrollback/history (e.g., `str(s.historybuf)` is non-empty or equals a non-empty expected string) after repeated draw + line-movement operations.
4.3: The top-margin test asserts that scrollback/history is unchanged/empty (e.g., `str(s.historybuf)` equals `''`) after repeated draw + line-movement operations.
4.4: The top-and-bottom-margins test asserts that scrollback/history is unchanged/empty (e.g., `str(s.historybuf)` equals `''`) after repeated draw + line-movement operations.
